tam_global.label: Total addressable market — padel software + AI-coaching software the user could ever buy
tam_global.growth_cagr: Padel club market projected to triple in value by 2026 vs 2023.
tam_global.basis: Global padel club market value 2023 EUR 1.775B → 2026 EUR 4.015B per Deloitte / Playtomic Global Padel Report 2023; 2025 figures confirm 35M players and 77,300 courts.
tam_global.verified_quote: global padel club market 'set to triple in value by 2026'
tam_global.story: If every one of the 35 million players spent USD 8/month on a coaching tool, the ceiling would be roughly USD 3.4 billion a year. The realistic ceiling sits at the share of that group who already pay for any padel-related digital service — Playtomic and MATCHi count multi-millions of monthly active users, so the player-software TAM is in the low single-digit billions of euros.

tam_ai_fitness.label: Adjacent ceiling — global AI in fitness and wellness market
tam_ai_fitness.basis: Global AI in fitness and wellness market sizing per InsightAce Analytic 2026.
tam_ai_fitness.verified_quote: valued at USD 10.68 billion in 2025, USD 57.80 billion by 2035
tam_ai_fitness.story: AI coaching is not a padel-only category. The wider AI fitness market — Strava-style, Whoop-style, Hudl-style products — is already at USD 10.7 billion globally. Padel taps an ~10× narrower slice today, but the slice grows fastest because padel is the fastest-growing racket sport in Europe.

tam_padel_market_growth.label: Padel-specific market growth — independent secondary
tam_padel_market_growth.verified_quote: USD 0.71 billion by 2035 from USD 0.32 billion in 2026
tam_padel_market_growth.story: A second analyst — Business Research Insights — sizes padel-only software smaller than Deloitte's club-and-services figure because they exclude court real estate. Both numbers move together, which is the validation that matters.

sam_priority_geographies.label: Serviceable addressable market — Spanish + Italian + French + Russian + LATAM Spanish
sam_priority_geographies.basis: Sum of FIP World Padel Report 2025 figures for these markets, multiplied by an EUR 22/year average software spend per active player (low end of Strava + booking-app combined ARPU).
sam_priority_geographies.verified_quote: Spain remains the best-equipped country with 17,300 courts.
sam_priority_geographies.story: Spain alone has 17,300 courts and 6 million players (FIP 2025). Italy hit 10,220 courts in 2025 with +12.9% YoY growth. France crossed 100,000 federation-licensed players for the first time. Add Argentina, Mexico, Portugal, Russia and Kazakhstan and the addressable population is roughly 17 million padel-active people — a market a serious software player can dominate without competing in every geography at once.

som_one_year.label: Realistic 1-year capture — smartphone-only rating product
som_one_year.basis: 0.03–0.12% of priority-geography active players paying EUR 7.99/month for 12 months. The low end matches what padel apps typically convert in year 1 against Playtomic's free booking layer; the high end matches Strava's early-cohort conversion rate scaled to padel's smaller paid base.
som_one_year.verified_quote: Padel insights by Playtomic — global active player benchmarks.
som_one_year.story: Concrete example: Playtomic's Spanish booking flow sees roughly 1.5 million monthly active users (Playtomic Global Padel Report 2025). If the rating product converts 0.5% of free trials to paid at EUR 7.99/month, year-one paid users land at ~7,500 and revenue at ~EUR 720,000. That number is bigger than zero, smaller than Wingfield, and exactly the size that proves product-market fit without depending on a venture cheque.
